SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects. Good visibility.  Winds 30 -40 mph, progress difficult and tiring.
Comment: Surface layers are expected to be firm and icy particularly where shaded from the sun.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight light snow expected around 1000 metres, rain at lower elevations. By dawn the freezing level will have dropped to around 800 metres but will rise again above most of the summits by late afternoon. Winds, will be variable throughout the period, strong overnight from the South-South-West veering to the North-West around dawn but easing significantly and backing to the South-Westerly by late afternoon.
SnowStability: New isolated accumulations will develop mostly around corrie rims and gully tops on North through East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. New deposits are expected to be shallow and generally inconsequential. Older snow, greatest on South-West to North-West aspects, will soften where exposed to the sun but remain well bonded throughout. Shaded areas will be firm and icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Wet and mild overnight followed by a dry, sunny day with high cloud cover. Around 2 degrees at 900 metres early afternoon. Gale force winds from the South-South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw at all levels but remains generally well bonded. Surface layers are soft, particularly where exposed to the sun but remain firm at higher levels and in shaded areas. Greatest snow amounts are found mainly on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 800 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Very firm snowpack.  Good visibility for most of the day.  Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: The snowpack will remain very firm with the potential for serious consequences in the event of a trip or slip.
WeatherInfluences: Light precipitation during the early part of the night will initially be rain at most levels turning to snow above around 700 metres as the freezing level falls and precipitation clears, a dry day will follow. The freezing level will fall to around 700 metres then gradually rise again to reach around 1200 metres by the end of the day. West-South-Westerly winds will be strong overnight gradually decreasing to become light.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will be well bonded, very firm and stable. There will be a superficial covering of new snow above around 700 metres, although new snow amounts are not expected to be significant.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry clear day with strong South-South-Westerly winds. The freezing level is around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. The snow surface is hard and icy in most locations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Limited patchy firm snow in specific places at high elevations.  Good visibility . Winds approx  50 mph on the tops,  progress difficult in gusts.
Comment: A bright day expected lighter winds but the old snow remains very firm with serious runouts for any falls.
WeatherInfluences: Winds will be strong West backing South-Westerly during the day.  Freezing level around 800 metres. Some showery precipitation falling as snow and sleet Monday evening but it is expected to be dry thereafter and during the day.
SnowStability: Little change, the patchy and limited snowpack that remains mostly above 750 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects will refreeze overnight, becoming very firm and stable throughout, although softening slightly later. There will be a dusting of new snow on the tops but amounts are not expected to be significant and will mostly disappear during the day as it warms.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry bright day with gale force South-Easterly winds. Quite cool in the wind, freezing level around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The old snowpack that now remains is well bonded and stable throughout. Although moist in the surface layers where exposed to the sun it remains firm at depth and in shaded areas. Snow cover is mostly patchy but significant areas of hard snow is present in some gullies, corrie backwalls  and summit slopes, mostly above 750 metres on North-West through North to East aspects.  The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice in many locations. Cloudy on the tops with bright spells on the Northern side of the plateau. Winds approx 60-75 mph at times with a gust of 100 mph recorded on Cairn Gorm overnight.
Comment: The snow surface will remain firm and consequential in the event of an uncontrolled slide. As a ridge of high pressure becomes established near the end of the period be alert to the snow surface remaining firm above 800 metres, in spite of a rising freezing level.
WeatherInfluences: Snow is expected above 1000 metres during the evening and first part of the night, with the potential for brief showers from 600 metres upwards. Winds will be initially South-South-Westerly and gale force, veering  West gradually decreasing to become moderate to light by the afternoon. The freezing level will be around 700 metres rising to 1250 metres by the end of the day.
SnowStability: Isolated unstable windslab will be deposited on North-West through North to East aspects above 950 metres. Snow amounts are expected to be modest, but deposits in steep areas such as gully tops and coire rims will be sensitive where they achieve greater depth. Outwith these areas the snowpack will be firm and stable. The snow surface will remain firm above 800 metres, softening below this and in areas exposed to solar radiation. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Precipitation overnight preceded a dry and bright day. Winds were very strong to gale force and from the South-South-West. The freezing level was around 1000 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm and stable on all aspects and elevations. The snow surface is very firm and icy on Northerly aspects and plateau areas above around 750 metres. Outwith these areas the snowpack consists of soft spring snow, particularly when exposed to solar radiation. Areas of bare ground are also present, with patchy cover on South-West aspects which have been exposed to overnight rain. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow. Good visibility. Winds 20 -30 mph.
Comment: The old snowpack will be firm and icy.
WeatherInfluences: Light snowfall above 950 metres is expected in the early part of the night. The freezing level will drop to 700 metres overnight and then rise steadily through the day to be above the summits. Very strong Southerly winds will veer Westerly and decrease to become moderate.
SnowStability: The snowpack will be firm and have good stability in all locations. Potential for fresh isolated deposits of wind drifted snow on North-East to North-West aspects above 1000 metres, although any accumulations are expected to be shallow. Snow is now generally confined to West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres, with only patches remaining below this level. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry and bright day with the freezing level briefly lowering to around 900 metres before rising to just below the summits. Fresh to strong South-South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has good stability in all locations. Following the overnight thaw, the snow surface became firm and icy above 900 metres. Below this elevation the snow was soft and continuing to diminish. Snow is now generally confined to West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres, with only patches remaining below this level. Plateau cover is extensive. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Snowpack firm and icy. Cloudy at first, visibility improving through day.  Winds 20 -30 mph progress.
Comment: Some uncertainty in snowfall amounts. If more snow than expected falls, then a possible windslab problem and  Moderate hazard in the areas where the windslab accumulates.
WeatherInfluences: After a few overnight snow showers a generally dry day is expected. South-Westerly winds will ease through the period. The freezing level will be around 800 metres.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will remain hard, icy and stable throughout. Some fresh windslab deposits are expected, mainly at the top of slopes and gullies with a North to East aspect above 900 metres. However, these deposits are likely to be shallow and generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with some good sunny spells. The freezing level was around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The previously wet snowpack has consolidated and frozen in the colder conditions. It is hard icy and stable at all levels. Stability is good throughout. The avalanche hazard is Low.
